title: Asyneuma leianthum
taxonomy: Asyneuma leianthum belongs to the family Campanulaceae. It is a species of flowering plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant is a member of the genus Asyneuma, which is a group of plants that are commonly known as harebells or bluebells. The classification of Asyneuma leianthum is further supported by its botanical name, which is a combination of its genus and species names. The classification of this plant is important for understanding its evolutionary relationships and for identifying it in the field.

intro: Asyneuma leianthum, also known as the mountain helichrysum, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide relief from pain and promote healing. The bioactive constituents of Asyneuma leianthum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Asyneuma leianthum include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
